riddle:
Here is the riddle with the corrected clues:

1. Seek the Wikipedia page for a key figure in the establishment of a sports movement emphasizing Jewish athleticism, known for founding a significant event in the early 20th century. Within his biography, note how old he was when he immigrated to Ottoman Palestine; that number will be your clue 1 — then follow the page's link to the international Maccabi federation to continue.

2. There is a linked page that represents the global federation of those clubs; on that federation's page determine the year in which it was formed — record that year as clue 2 — next, search for the article about the inaugural Games.

3. Which nations count is listed in the opening-ceremony section of the inaugural Games' article — find how many countries participated and make that figure clue 3 — from that article follow the link to the stadium used at the Games.

4. If you open the stadium's page, look up how high above sea level the host city stands (seek the city's elevation) and take that elevation in meters as clue 4 — you will use it in a transformation shortly.

5. The article whose opening lists that elevation yields the raw number you just found; convert clue 4 into binary and count how many 1-bits it contains — that count is your clue 5 (the hint: count the ones in the binary representation of clue 4).

6. In the movement's history, the aforementioned figure proposed holding the first Games to mark a particular anniversary of an ancient revolt; from the history section of the movement's overview find the anniversary in years he targeted and record that number as clue 6 — then look up notation for mapping coordinates.

7. The globe whispers locations using compact notation; on the page about geodetic coordinate notation, read the figure and state the symbol pair used to denote a single point (the form shown in the illustrative figure) — that notation text is clue 7 — next, seek the riverside power compound at the Jordan bend.

8. (On the map, two historical sites face one another.) Calculate the driving distance between the riverside power compound at the Jordan's bend and the Maccabiah athletes' village used in the early Games; the driving distance you obtain (in the units shown) becomes clue 8.

9. Now take the distance number recorded as clue 8 and reverse its digits to form a new integer — the reversed number is clue 9 (hint: simply read the digits of clue 8 from last to first).

10. Nearby searches can reveal local services; perform a map search around the hilltop fort that overlooks the Hula valley and count how many hospitals appear in the results — that total is clue 10.

11. Finally, compute the single-digit result of this formula and report it: (clue 2 + clue 5 + clue 8) % 10 — your answer must be a single digit from 0 to 9.

metadata.theme: From the founder of the Maccabiah Games to the maps that place them: a short trail linking Yosef Yekutieli and the early Maccabiah movement to the geographic tools (geodesy and geocoding) used to locate and compare sporting venues and historic sites.
